title: AEP v1.1 F16 AttackClass Registry Entry
description: v1.1 F16 frontier-break primitive (SPECULATIVE FRONTIER). Three-agent convergence (adversary+judge+forge). AttackClass claim type + inverse attack_classes_closed[] on assertions; bidirectional index. Every prior pre-mortem becomes a live attack-detector on every new claim emit. Composes_with: v0.8 F5 self_falsifying, v1.0.3 RegexicalCue (attack-class to cue resolution), sec02 truth-tags, sec50 EH Law-3.

attack_class_id.description: Human-readable attack-class identifier. Recommended: ATK-V<spec-version>-<letter><number>. Example: ATK-V80-N1.

attack_class_name.description: Short title for the attack class. Recommended: 4-8 words.

attack_signature_regex.description: Portable regex (rxmem-v1 dialect or PCRE-compat) used to detect the attack signature in new claim text or artifact bodies. Validator runs this against claim emit candidates BEFORE truth-tag assignment.

registered_by_principal.description: Principal that registered this attack class (typically an adversary or judge role).

detection_runtime.description: Runtime used to evaluate attack_signature_regex. f9_quorum = run on N>=2 substrates per v1.0.x F9.

first_seen_in_claim_id.description: Optional. Claim ID where this attack signature was first observed (the canonical premortem instance).

closed_by_packets.description: Packet IDs that close this attack class (inverse of attack_classes_closed[] on their assertions). Forms the bidirectional index.

severity.description: Adversary verdict tier per legion v2 conventions. HIGH-VETO is non-rescindable per sec69.4.

composes_with_doctrine_slots.description: Doctrine slot IDs this attack class composes with.

false_positive_rate_observed.description: Empirically observed FPR on corpus runs. Default null (unmeasured). Validator emits AEP11_F16_FPR_UNMEASURED warning if null after >=5 corpus runs.

narrative_description.description: Human-readable description of the attack class. Cited by validator emit messages.

examples.narrative_description: Lodestone V3 shipped 658 LOC calling window.claude.complete which does not exist. F16 detects this pattern at claim-emit time.
